D9 BiologyL3 ApplyReference

Fruit Ripening — Chlorophyll Out, Lycopene In

a green tomato decays chlorophyll while synthesising lycopene; ethylene-driven biochemistry shifts the colour from chlorophyll-green to lycopene-red in a few days

Tomato ripening is the canonical study: ethylene gas triggers chlorophyll catabolism + lycopene biosynthesis simultaneously. Chlorophyll concentration drops exponentially; lycopene rises sigmoidally. Same fruit, days later, completely different colour. The same machinery governs banana, mango, persimmon.

Drag ripening day; tomato swatch transitions green → yellow → red.
